title: Python Programming Learning App

requirements.text: Create a backend actor that stores Python programming lessons, each with a title, description, code snippet (string), difficulty level (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced), and a unique ID. Provide functions to: list all lessons, get a lesson by ID, and add new lessons. Pre-populate with at least 8 diverse Python lessons covering topics such as variables, loops, functions, list comprehensions, dictionaries, classes, error handling, and file I/O.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores lessons with id, title, description, codeSnippet, difficulty, and topic fields
getLessons() returns all lessons
getLesson(id) returns a single lesson
At least 8 pre-populated Python lessons are available on first load

requirements.text: Create a backend actor function to store and retrieve user progress: which lesson IDs the user has marked as completed. Provide markComplete(lessonId) and getProgress() functions.
acceptanceCriteria: markComplete(lessonId) persists a completed lesson ID
getProgress() returns the list of completed lesson IDs
Progress persists across page reloads

requirements.text: Build a lesson browser page showing all available Python lessons as cards. Each card displays the lesson title, topic tag, difficulty badge, and a completion checkmark if the user has completed it. Include a filter bar to filter lessons by difficulty level (All, Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced).
acceptanceCriteria: All lessons are displayed as cards on the main page
Cards show title, topic, difficulty badge, and completion indicator
Filtering by difficulty correctly shows/hides cards
Progress summary (e.g., '3 of 8 completed') is visible

requirements.text: Build a lesson detail view that opens when a user clicks a lesson card. The detail view displays the full lesson description and a syntax-highlighted Python code snippet using a monospace code block with line numbers. Include a 'Mark as Complete' button that updates the user's progress in the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a lesson card opens its detail view
Code snippet is displayed in a styled, readable monospace block with a distinct code background
Python keywords are visually distinguished (bold or colored) in the code block
'Mark as Complete' button calls the backend and updates the UI state immediately
Button label changes to 'Completed ✓' after marking

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive dark-themed visual design inspired by code editors: deep charcoal/dark slate backgrounds, bright green or amber accent colors for highlights and CTAs, monospace fonts for code blocks, and a clean sans-serif font for UI text. Use consistent card layouts, subtle borders, and glowing accent effects on interactive elements.
acceptanceCriteria: App uses a dark background color scheme throughout
Accent color is consistently applied to badges, buttons, and highlights
Code blocks use a monospace font and a distinguishable background
UI feels cohesive and polished across all pages

requirements.text: Generate and display a logo image for the app header: a stylized Python snake icon intertwined with code brackets on a dark background, used as the app's branding mark in the top navigation bar.
acceptanceCriteria: Logo image is displayed in the top navigation/header area
Image is loaded from frontend/public/assets/generated/
Logo is clearly visible against the dark header background

constraints: Do not attempt to execute Python code at runtime — the platform runs Motoko on the backend only
All Python code examples must be stored as static strings in the backend
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Live Python code execution or a REPL environment
User authentication or login (Internet Identity not required for this app)

required: Stylized Python snake coiled around angle brackets (< >) with a glowing green tint on a dark charcoal background (pylearn-logo.dim_128x128.png)
